versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: He will be raised and lifted up and highly exalted.
14 Just as many were appalled at Him—
His appearance was disfigured beyond that of any man,
and His form was marred beyond human likeness—
15 so He will sprinkle many nations.
Kings will shut their mouths because of Him.
For they will see what they have not been told,
and they will understand what they have not heard.

context_lines: In **Isaiah 52**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Isaiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **The Servant Exalted** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Isaiah 52** remains the first control on meaning.
